academic_requirements.summary: A minimum three-year bachelor's degree or equivalent is required. The current 2026/2027 programme page requires at least 60 CFU in business, economics, law, and statistical-mathematical fields and assesses personal preparation through CV/studiorum, a written test, and possibly an interview. The current apply.unive.it international course page also states a minimum CGPA of 70% [uncertain].
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree equivalent to an Italian three-year bachelor's degree (at least 3 years / 180 ECTS or equivalent).
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Candidates must have at least 60 CFU/ECTS in the following areas: Business (SECS-P/07, SECS-P/08, SECS-P/09, SECS-P/10, SECS-P/11), Economics (SECS-P/01, SECS-P/02, SECS-P/03, SECS-P/04, SECS-P/05, SECS-P/06, SECS-P/12), Law (IUS/01, IUS/04, IUS/05, IUS/07, IUS/09, IUS/10, IUS/12, IUS/13, IUS/14), and Statistical-Mathematics (MAT/02, MAT/03, MAT/05, MAT/06, MAT/08, MAT/09, SECS-S/01, SECS-S/03, SECS-S/06).
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: [uncertain] The current apply.unive.it international course page states a minimum CGPA of 70%. The current 2026/2027 unive.it programme admission page does not restate a CGPA threshold.
academic_requirements.committee_or_pre_evaluation: Applicants with international qualifications must first pass the Welcome Unit preliminary evaluation on apply.unive.it. The Teaching Committee then evaluates curriculum vitae and studiorum; depending on the case, a written test and possibly an additional interview are used to assess personal preparation.

accepted_proofs: A recognised English certification accepted by Ca' Foscari and submitted within 5 years from the certification date.

Examples on the official recognised list include Cambridge English FCE, CAE and CPE; Trinity ISE II-IV; IELTS; TOEFL iBT; PTE Academic; Oxford Test of English; LanguageCert ESOL; Aptis ESOL; and Ca' Foscari CLA English certificates.
exemption_rules: Completed higher education studies, or at least one year of college, in a country where English is the medium of instruction.
Obtained a high-school diploma or university qualification in Italy or abroad after studies taught entirely in English.
Completed at least one full exchange year in high school at an institution where English was the language of instruction.
Holds one of the specific Italian university language degrees listed by Ca' Foscari for English-language exemption.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Upload the certificate electronically during the application/enrolment process. The programme page says applicants may apply without the certificate, but they cannot enrol if they do not satisfy the requirement by the enrolment deadline.

rounds.notes: Official evaluation session on the current 2026/2027 programme page.
Official evaluation session on the current 2026/2027 programme page.
Official evaluation session on the current 2026/2027 programme page.

application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Standard enrolment: July 1, 2026 to September 30, 2026. Additional late period: October 1, 2026 to December 18, 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: [uncertain] For international-qualification applicants who fall into the EU/equivalent category, the public apply.unive.it article gives a final master's application deadline of June 17, 2026, but its accessible text does not expose the detailed round breakdown. The programme page above gives the detailed 2026/2027 evaluation sessions and enrolment windows.

rounds.round_name: 2026/2027 master's admission rounds for non-EU applicants requiring a study visa
rounds.application_window: [uncertain] The public visa-applicant article does not expose the individual round windows in the accessible text.
rounds.deadline: As of June 5, 2026, the official article states that it is no longer possible to apply for admission to a bachelor's or master's degree in 2026/2027.
rounds.notes: Non-EU applicants already residing in Italy with a permit valid for enrolment are instructed to refer instead to the EU/equivalent admission-rounds article.

application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: [uncertain] Not stated in the accessible public visa-applicant article.
application_deadline_non_eu.current_status: Closed as of June 5, 2026 according to the official apply.unive.it article for visa-requiring applicants.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The public source provides status rather than a round-by-round schedule.

required_documents.document_name: Valid passport or valid ID card
required_documents.notes: European applicants may use an ID card; non-European applicants use a passport.

required_documents.document_name: Valid residence permit or receipt of renewal/first request
required_documents.notes: Required for non-European applicants already residing in Italy or in the Schengen Area.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records for all undergraduate years
required_documents.notes: A provisional transcript is acceptable if the applicant is still attending the final year.

required_documents.document_name: Official English or Italian translations of transcript and diploma documents
required_documents.notes: Required when the original documents are not in English or Italian.

required_documents.document_name: Grading scale of the university CGPA
required_documents.notes: Listed as highly recommended on the official international-entry-requirements page.
Used by the Teaching Committee when assessing personal preparation.
The current apply.unive.it course page requires a dedicated motivation letter for this master's degree.
Optional during the application stage if not yet available, but required later for final enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: CIMEA Statement of Comparability, or Declaration of Value, or ARDI Statement of Correspondence with CIMEA Statement of Verification, or Diploma Supplement
required_documents.notes: These documents are strongly recommended during application and are mandatory later to complete enrolment.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: [uncertain] Online written test for personal preparation assessment
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Most applicants. The current 2026/2027 programme page grants exemptions to candidates with specified bachelor's degrees and a minimum final grade of 95/110, and to certain master's-degree holders. The current apply.unive.it international course page states that all candidates must take the test.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Current 2026/2027 programme page: June 16, 2026; September 7, 2026; and November 30, 2026, all at 10:00 AM and held remotely via Zoom/Moodle.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: [uncertain] The unive.it programme page says candidates with L-18, L-33, or L-41 (or equivalent) and a final grade of 95/110 are exempt, and certain master's-degree holders with 95/110 may also request exemption. The apply.unive.it course page instead says all candidates must take the test.

source_quotes.quote: The Economics, Finance and Sustainability master's degree programme is an open admission programme taught in English.
a minimum CGPA (Cumulative Grade Point Average) of 70%
A minimum B2-level knowledge of English is mandatory for enrolment.
A motivation letter must be added to your application.

uncertainty_notes: The current apply.unive.it international course page states a minimum CGPA of 70%, but the current 2026/2027 unive.it programme admission page does not restate this threshold.

The current 2026/2027 unive.it programme admission page gives exemption rules for the written test, while the current apply.unive.it international course page says all candidates must take an online written entry test.

For the EU/equivalent international-applicant route, the public apply.unive.it article gives a final master's deadline of June 17, 2026, but its accessible text does not expose the round-by-round schedule.
The public apply.unive.it article for visa-requiring applicants gives current closure status, but not a detailed visa round schedule or a specific visa/pre-enrolment deadline.
